inputs.per_share_source: Q1 2026 Distributable Earnings per share $0.24 (SEVN-Q1-2026-Earnings-Presentation.pdf, p.5) annualized x4
inputs.normalization_note: Q1 2026 DE/share of $0.24 (117% payout ratio) reflects temporary dilution from the December 2025 rights offering (7.5M new shares, weighted average shares roughly +50% versus a year earlier) with new capital not yet fully deployed. Q4 2025 DE/share was $0.28 (100% payout coverage) before the raise. Cash on hand fell from $123.5M (Q4 2025) to $56.6M (Q1 2026) as $67.5M of that capital was already put to work; $44.0M of unfunded commitments and $397.5M of unused financing capacity remain to deploy. Base case models a return toward the $0.28/quarter run rate over the next 18-24 months as this capital is invested at the current ~7.8% weighted average all-in yield.
inputs.manager: Tremont Realty Capital (wholly owned subsidiary of The RMR Group)

bear.notes: Renewed CRE stress (office concentration in particular) forces higher provisioning and at least one dividend cut; new capital redeploys slowly or into lower-yielding, more conservative loans; market keeps SEVN at a structural discount to book (~0.55x).

base.notes: Rights-offering capital (cash + unfunded commitments) redeploys over roughly two years back to the $0.28/quarter run rate, then grows slowly with the floating-rate book; no realized credit losses; discount to book narrows modestly as the sector re-rates toward ~0.75-0.85x book (about 9x distributable earnings).

bull.notes: Full, faster capital deployment plus a rate environment that holds financing spreads wide; CRE credit fear fades faster than base case; multiple re-rates toward par book value (~1.0x, about 12x distributable earnings) as peers with cleaner books trade there today.

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

evidence_refresh.note: Total synthesis IRR is computed and shown as a separate capstone block per irr_assumption_ledger.md; the Lawrence scenario base stays the headline base_pct until human_approval on synthesis weights.

stance_proposal.override_reason: Marvin filings primary (Lawrence base 14.6%, not the 16.04% auto-synthesis, which lacks a filing_falsifier anchor path under the scenario method and is pending human_approval). Moat unproven: externally managed by Tremont/RMR with a 1.5% base + 20% incentive fee structure and no scale or underwriting moat versus mortgage REIT peers. Dhando partial: high current income (13.3% dividend yield) and low weighted average LTV (66%) cap much of the downside, but a persistent price-to-book discount and CRE-sector credit fear are real risks, so full dhando is not asserted.

valuation_method_route.label: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
failure_modes: replacement cost used for assets that cannot earn their cost of capital
valuation_method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

falsifiers: Credit losses or mark-to-market force book below the low case.
Fee load or internalization cost proves larger than the low case.
Realized losses or failed redeployment push economic equity below the low case.
Dividend cut and DE permanently below coverage invalidate income-capitalization cross-checks.
required_refresh_triggers: new filing or material operating update

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

universal_valuation_contract.decision_rule: No security is decision-grade until every material claim is valued exactly once by a valid calculation proof and every material evidence blocker is resolved.

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

qualitative_adjustments.factor: No dated predictive attribute at spot
qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Scarcity is descriptive; no timed mispricing catalyst in base
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Cross-check names dated catalyst with timeline

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative

valuation_methodology.decision_rule: Use one complete component schedule (loan book equity, earnings franchise, fee drag, credit reserve). Lawrence scenario IRR remains the stance-gate return.

component_valuation.coverage_statement: Mortgage-REIT equity is book claim less fee drag and credit/redeployment reserves. Earnings franchise is embedded in the book capitalization cross-check.

wisdom_applied: Stahl/HK: price-to-book discounts in levered credit vehicles can overshoot when credit is actually clean — verify the loan book, don't average the sector.
Munger: fee structure and incentives are part of the intrinsic claim.
limitations: Lawrence scenario base 14.6% remains the stance-gate return until synthesis weights get human approval.
No approved third-party CRE sector source in base IRR.

component_valuation_results.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key cre_credit_and_redeployment_reserve; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Realized losses or failed redeployment push economic equity below the low case.

valuation_proof.evidence: Q1 2026 DE $0.24/sh (temp dilution); Q4 2025 was $0.28/sh. Embedded in how the market capitalizes book — treated as embedded so not double-counted with book equity.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Marks, ACL, and leverage are inside book.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key tangible_loan_book_equity; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Dividend cut and DE permanently below coverage invalidate income-capitalization cross-checks.

wisdom_applied: Stahl/HK: price-to-book discounts in levered credit vehicles can overshoot when credit is actually clean — verify the loan book, don't average the sector.
Munger: fee structure and incentives are part of the intrinsic claim.
limitations: Lawrence scenario base 14.6% remains the stance-gate return until synthesis weights get human approval.
No approved third-party CRE sector source in base IRR.

component_review_queue.decision_rule: Each assigned rater records accept, challenge, or reject with a proposed range and evidence. The aggregator may not silently average disputed ranges.

items.label: Tangible loan-book equity (adjusted book)
mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?
Is the security's ownership claim and economic-unit denominator reconciled correctly?
Does the evidence support the low/base/high range and its timing?
Are comparable assets truly like-for-like after production, contract, location, capital, ownership, and realization adjustments?
For an option, are success probability, time to realization, and remaining owner-funded capital explicit?
Is the component additive, or already embedded in another component?
What observation would move the base estimate materially?
